title: What does HTML stand for?
description: Choose the correct expansion of HTML.
correct_answer: HyperText Markup Language

title: What does CSS stand for?
description: What is the full form of CSS?
correct_answer: Cascading Style Sheets

title: What is a div tag?
description: What is the purpose of the <div> tag?
options: A block-level container for grouping content
correct_answer: A block-level container for grouping content

title: What is a span tag?
description: What is the <span> tag used for?
options: An inline container for marking up text
correct_answer: An inline container for marking up text

title: What is the <a> tag?
description: What does the anchor tag do?
options: Creates a hyperlink to another page or resource
correct_answer: Creates a hyperlink to another page or resource

title: What is the tag?
description: What does the img tag do?
options: Embeds an image in the page
correct_answer: Embeds an image in the page

title: What is semantic HTML?
options: Elements that clearly describe their meaning (e.g., <header>, <nav>, <article>)
correct_answer: Elements that clearly describe their meaning (e.g., <header>, <nav>, <article>)

title: What is the <head> section?
description: What goes in the <head> of an HTML document?
options: Metadata, title, links to stylesheets, and scripts
correct_answer: Metadata, title, links to stylesheets, and scripts

title: What is the <body> section?
options: All visible content of the web page
correct_answer: All visible content of the web page

title: What is the <title> tag?
options: Sets the title shown in the browser tab
Creates a heading on the page
correct_answer: Sets the title shown in the browser tab

title: What is an unordered list?
description: How do you create an unordered list?
correct_answer: Using <ul> and <li> tags

title: What is an ordered list?
description: How do you create an ordered list?
correct_answer: Using <ol> and <li> tags

title: What is the <form> element?
options: Creates an interactive form for user input
correct_answer: Creates an interactive form for user input

title: What is the <input> element?
options: An interactive control for forms (text, checkbox, radio, etc.)
correct_answer: An interactive control for forms (text, checkbox, radio, etc.)

title: What is the <button> element?
correct_answer: A clickable button

title: What is the <table> element?
options: A table for displaying tabular data
correct_answer: A table for displaying tabular data

title: What is the CSS color property?
description: What does the color property style?
options: The text color of an element
correct_answer: The text color of an element

title: What is the background-color property?
options: Sets the background color of an element
correct_answer: Sets the background color of an element

title: What is the font-size property?
correct_answer: The size of the text

title: What is the margin property?
correct_answer: Space outside the element's border

title: What is the padding property?
correct_answer: Space inside the element's border

title: What is the border property?
description: What does the border shorthand set?
options: Width, style, and color of the element's border
correct_answer: Width, style, and color of the element's border

title: What is display: none?
options: Removes the element from the layout entirely
Hides the element but keeps its space
correct_answer: Removes the element from the layout entirely

title: What is visibility: hidden?
description: How does visibility: hidden differ from display: none?
options: Element is invisible but still takes up space
correct_answer: Element is invisible but still takes up space

title: What is the class selector?
description: How do you select elements by class in CSS?
correct_answer: Using a dot prefix: .classname

title: What is the ID selector?
description: How do you select an element by ID?
correct_answer: Using a hash: #idname

title: What is an inline style?
description: How do you apply an inline style?
options: Using the style attribute: style="color: red"
correct_answer: Using the style attribute: style="color: red"

title: What is the <link> tag?
options: Links an external CSS stylesheet to the HTML document
correct_answer: Links an external CSS stylesheet to the HTML document

title: What is text-align?
options: Sets the horizontal alignment of text
correct_answer: Sets the horizontal alignment of text

title: What is width and height?
description: What do width and height set?
options: The dimensions of an element's content area
correct_answer: The dimensions of an element's content area

title: What is the <h1> to <h6> tags?
options: Headings of decreasing importance/size from h1 (largest) to h6 (smallest)
correct_answer: Headings of decreasing importance/size from h1 (largest) to h6 (smallest)

title:
What is the

tag?
correct_answer: A paragraph of text

title:
What is the
tag?
correct_answer: Inserts a line break

title: What is the <strong> tag?
options: Indicates strong importance (displayed as bold by default)
correct_answer: Indicates strong importance (displayed as bold by default)

title: What is the <em> tag?
options: Indicates emphasis (displayed as italic by default)
correct_answer: Indicates emphasis (displayed as italic by default)

title: What is font-family?
correct_answer: The typeface used for text

title: What is line-height?
options: The vertical space between lines of text
correct_answer: The vertical space between lines of text

title: What is the <meta> tag?
options: Metadata about the HTML document (charset, viewport, description)
correct_answer: Metadata about the HTML document (charset, viewport, description)

title: What is the <!DOCTYPE> declaration?
options: Declares the document type as HTML5
correct_answer: Declares the document type as HTML5

title: What is the alt attribute?
description: What does the alt attribute on provide?
options: Alternative text for screen readers and when the image fails to load
correct_answer: Alternative text for screen readers and when the image fails to load

title: What is the href attribute?
options: The URL that a link points to
correct_answer: The URL that a link points to

title: What is the target='_blank' attribute?
description: What does target='_blank' do on a link?
options: Opens the linked page in a new tab/window
correct_answer: Opens the linked page in a new tab/window

title: What is position: relative?
options: Positions the element relative to its normal position
Fixes the element to the viewport
Positions relative to the nearest ancestor
correct_answer: Positions the element relative to its normal position

title: What is position: absolute?
options: Positions relative to the nearest positioned ancestor
Positions relative to the body always
correct_answer: Positions relative to the nearest positioned ancestor

title: What is position: fixed?
options: Positions the element relative to the viewport (stays on scroll)
correct_answer: Positions the element relative to the viewport (stays on scroll)

title: What is position: sticky?
options: Acts like relative until a scroll threshold, then acts like fixed
correct_answer: Acts like relative until a scroll threshold, then acts like fixed

title: What is the <select> element?
options: A dropdown menu for choosing options
correct_answer: A dropdown menu for choosing options

title: What is the <textarea> element?
correct_answer: A multi-line text input field

title: What is the box model?
description: What are the parts of the CSS box model?
correct_answer: Content, padding, border, margin

title: What is the <video> tag?
options: Embeds video content in the page
correct_answer: Embeds video content in the page

title: What is the <audio> tag?
options: Embeds audio content in the page
correct_answer: Embeds audio content in the page

title: What is the placeholder attribute?
description: What does placeholder do on an input?
options: Displays hint text that disappears when the user types
correct_answer: Displays hint text that disappears when the user types

title: What is overflow: hidden?
options: Clips content that overflows the element's box
correct_answer: Clips content that overflows the element's box

title: What is z-index?
options: The stacking order of positioned elements
correct_answer: The stacking order of positioned elements

title: What is opacity?
description: What does the opacity property do?
options: Sets the transparency level (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)
correct_answer: Sets the transparency level (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)

title: What is the cursor property?
options: Changes the mouse cursor to a hand pointer
correct_answer: Changes the mouse cursor to a hand pointer

title: What is the float property?
options: Places an element to the left or right, allowing text to wrap around it
correct_answer: Places an element to the left or right, allowing text to wrap around it

title: What is the clear property?
options: Specifies that an element should not wrap around floating elements
correct_answer: Specifies that an element should not wrap around floating elements

title: What is text-decoration?
options: Adds decorations like underline, overline, or line-through to text
correct_answer: Adds decorations like underline, overline, or line-through to text

title: What is Flexbox?
description: What is CSS Flexbox used for?
options: A one-dimensional layout system for arranging items in rows or columns
correct_answer: A one-dimensional layout system for arranging items in rows or columns

title: What is CSS Grid?
options: A two-dimensional layout system for rows and columns simultaneously
correct_answer: A two-dimensional layout system for rows and columns simultaneously

title: What is justify-content?
description: What does justify-content do in Flexbox?
options: Aligns items along the main axis
Aligns items along the cross axis
correct_answer: Aligns items along the main axis

title: What is align-items?
description: What does align-items do in Flexbox?
options: Aligns items along the cross axis
Aligns items along the main axis
correct_answer: Aligns items along the cross axis

title: What is flex-direction?
options: The direction of the main axis (row, row-reverse, column, column-reverse)
correct_answer: The direction of the main axis (row, row-reverse, column, column-reverse)

title: What is flex-wrap?
options: Whether flex items wrap onto multiple lines
correct_answer: Whether flex items wrap onto multiple lines

title: What is the gap property?
description: What does gap do in Flex/Grid?
options: Sets the spacing between flex/grid items
correct_answer: Sets the spacing between flex/grid items

title: What is grid-template-columns?
options: The number and size of columns in a grid container
correct_answer: The number and size of columns in a grid container

title: What is the fr unit?
description: What does 1fr mean in CSS Grid?
options: One fraction of the available space in the grid container
correct_answer: One fraction of the available space in the grid container

title: What is a media query?
options: Apply styles based on device characteristics (width, height, orientation)
correct_answer: Apply styles based on device characteristics (width, height, orientation)

title: What is responsive design?
description: What is the goal of responsive design?
options: Making websites adapt to different screen sizes and devices
correct_answer: Making websites adapt to different screen sizes and devices

title: What is the viewport meta tag?
description: What does the viewport meta tag control?
options: The visible area of a web page on mobile devices (scaling, width)
correct_answer: The visible area of a web page on mobile devices (scaling, width)

title: What is CSS specificity?
correct_answer: Inline styles > IDs > Classes/Attributes > Elements (e.g., 1,0,0,0 > 0,1,0,0)

title: What is the CSS cascade?
description: How does the cascade determine which styles apply?
options: Origin, specificity, and source order determine which declaration wins
correct_answer: Origin, specificity, and source order determine which declaration wins

title: What is the !important declaration?
options: Overrides all other declarations regardless of specificity
correct_answer: Overrides all other declarations regardless of specificity

title: What are CSS variables?
description: How do you declare a CSS custom property?
correct_answer: --variable-name: value; accessed with var(--variable-name)

title: What is a pseudo-class?
options: Applies styles when the user hovers over an element
correct_answer: Applies styles when the user hovers over an element

title: What is a pseudo-element?
options: Inserts content before an element's content
correct_answer: Inserts content before an element's content

title: What is the transform property?
options: Applies 2D or 3D transformations (rotate, scale, translate, skew)
correct_answer: Applies 2D or 3D transformations (rotate, scale, translate, skew)

title: What is the transition property?
options: Smoothly animates changes between CSS property values
correct_answer: Smoothly animates changes between CSS property values

title: What is the animation property?
options: Using @keyframes to define states and the animation property to apply them
correct_answer: Using @keyframes to define states and the animation property to apply them

title: What is box-sizing: border-box?
options: Includes padding and border in the element's total width and height
correct_answer: Includes padding and border in the element's total width and height

title: What is the calc() function?
description: What does calc() do in CSS?
options: Performs calculations to determine CSS property values (e.g., width: calc(100% - 20px))
correct_answer: Performs calculations to determine CSS property values (e.g., width: calc(100% - 20px))

title: What are rem and em units?
description: How do rem and em differ?
options: rem is relative to the root font-size; em is relative to the parent's font-size
correct_answer: rem is relative to the root font-size; em is relative to the parent's font-size

title: What is the clamp() function?
description: What does clamp(min, preferred, max) do?
options: Sets a value that's clamped between a minimum and maximum
Clamps an element to the page
correct_answer: Sets a value that's clamped between a minimum and maximum

title: What is the :nth-child selector?
correct_answer: Every even child element

title: What is the :focus-visible pseudo-class?
options: Applies styles only when focus is visible (keyboard navigation), not on mouse click
correct_answer: Applies styles only when focus is visible (keyboard navigation), not on mouse click

title: What is the object-fit property?
options: How replaced content (images, videos) is resized to fit its container
correct_answer: How replaced content (images, videos) is resized to fit its container

title: What is the aspect-ratio property?
options: Sets a preferred aspect ratio for an element (e.g., 16/9)
correct_answer: Sets a preferred aspect ratio for an element (e.g., 16/9)

title: What is the :has() selector?
options: Selects a parent element that contains a specific child or condition
correct_answer: Selects a parent element that contains a specific child or condition

title: What is the will-change property?
options: Hints to the browser about properties that will animate, enabling GPU optimization
Changes the will of the element
correct_answer: Hints to the browser about properties that will animate, enabling GPU optimization

title: What is the @supports rule?
options: Conditionally applies CSS based on browser support for a feature
correct_answer: Conditionally applies CSS based on browser support for a feature

title: What is ARIA?
correct_answer: Accessible Rich Internet Applications

title: What is the role attribute?
description: What does the role attribute do?
options: Defines the element's purpose for assistive technologies
correct_answer: Defines the element's purpose for assistive technologies

title: What is the <canvas> element?
options: A drawable surface for rendering graphics via JavaScript
correct_answer: A drawable surface for rendering graphics via JavaScript

title: What is the <picture> element?
options: Provides multiple image sources for different viewport conditions
correct_answer: Provides multiple image sources for different viewport conditions

title: What is the Container Query?
description: What do CSS container queries allow?
options: Styling elements based on the size of their container rather than the viewport
correct_answer: Styling elements based on the size of their container rather than the viewport

title: What is the scroll-behavior property?
options: Enables smooth scrolling animation when navigating to page anchors
correct_answer: Enables smooth scrolling animation when navigating to page anchors

title: Explain the stacking context in CSS.
description: What creates a new stacking context?
options: Elements with position + z-index, opacity < 1, transform, filter, or will-change create new stacking contexts
correct_answer: Elements with position + z-index, opacity < 1, transform, filter, or will-change create new stacking contexts

title: How does the CSS contain property work?
options: Limits the scope of browser's layout, style, and paint work for performance optimization
correct_answer: Limits the scope of browser's layout, style, and paint work for performance optimization

title: What is the CSS content-visibility property?
options: Skips rendering of off-screen content, dramatically improving initial load performance
correct_answer: Skips rendering of off-screen content, dramatically improving initial load performance

title: Explain CSS Houdini.
options: Low-level APIs to extend CSS (Paint API, Layout API, Properties and Values API)
correct_answer: Low-level APIs to extend CSS (Paint API, Layout API, Properties and Values API)

title: How does the browser rendering pipeline work?
description: What are the stages of rendering?
options: Parse HTML → Build DOM → Parse CSS → Build CSSOM → Layout → Paint → Composite
correct_answer: Parse HTML → Build DOM → Parse CSS → Build CSSOM → Layout → Paint → Composite

title: What is the Critical Rendering Path?
options: The sequence of steps the browser takes to convert HTML, CSS, and JS into pixels. Render-blocking CSS and JS delay it.
correct_answer: The sequence of steps the browser takes to convert HTML, CSS, and JS into pixels. Render-blocking CSS and JS delay it.

title: What is layout thrashing?
options: Repeatedly reading and writing DOM properties, forcing the browser to recalculate layout synchronously
correct_answer: Repeatedly reading and writing DOM properties, forcing the browser to recalculate layout synchronously

title: What is the difference between reflow and repaint?
options: Reflow recalculates layout (geometry); repaint redraws pixels without layout changes. Reflow is more expensive.
correct_answer: Reflow recalculates layout (geometry); repaint redraws pixels without layout changes. Reflow is more expensive.

title: How does CSS Grid subgrid work?
options: A nested grid that inherits track sizing from its parent grid, enabling alignment across nested levels
correct_answer: A nested grid that inherits track sizing from its parent grid, enabling alignment across nested levels

title: What is the @layer rule?
options: Allow control over the cascade order of CSS rules, managing specificity conflicts between different sources
correct_answer: Allow control over the cascade order of CSS rules, managing specificity conflicts between different sources

title: Build a responsive navbar.
description: Write HTML and CSS for a responsive navbar that collapses into a hamburger menu on mobile screens (max-width: 768px). Include logo, nav links, and a toggleable menu.
correct_answer:
HTML:
Brand
  • Home
  • About
  • Contact
CSS: .navbar { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; padding: 1rem; } .nav-links { display: flex; list-style: none; gap: 1rem; } .toggle { display: none; } @media (max-width: 768px) { .toggle { display: block; } .nav-links { display: none; flex-direction: column; } .nav-links.active { display: flex; } }

title: Create a CSS Grid layout.
description: Write CSS to create a responsive grid layout with a header spanning full width, a sidebar (250px), main content (1fr), and a footer spanning full width. On mobile, stack vertically.
correct_answer: .container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 250px 1fr; grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto; grid-template-areas: 'header header' 'sidebar main' 'footer footer'; min-height: 100vh; } .header { grid-area: header; } .sidebar { grid-area: sidebar; } .main { grid-area: main; } .footer { grid-area: footer; } @media (max-width: 768px) { .container { grid-template-columns: 1fr; grid-template-areas: 'header' 'main' 'sidebar' 'footer'; } }

title: Build a card component with Flexbox.
description: Write HTML and CSS for a card component with an image on top, title, description, and a button at the bottom. The button should always stick to the bottom regardless of content height. Use Flexbox.
correct_answer:
HTML:

Title

Description text goes here

CSS: .card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; width: 300px; border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .card-img { width: 100%; height: 200px; object-fit: cover; } .card-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex: 1; padding: 1rem; } .card-body p { flex: 1; } .card-btn { align-self: flex-start; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; }

title: Implement a CSS-only tooltip.
description: Create a tooltip that appears on hover using only HTML and CSS. The tooltip should appear above the element with an arrow pointing down.
correct_answer: HTML: Hover me CSS: .tooltip { position: relative; cursor: pointer; } .tooltip::after { content: attr(data-tip); position: absolute; bottom: 100%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); padding: 0.5rem; background: #333; color: #fff; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.8rem; white-space: nowrap; opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .tooltip::before { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 100%; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); border: 5px solid transparent; border-top-color: #333; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .tooltip:hover::after, .tooltip:hover::before { opacity: 1; }

title: Create a CSS loading spinner animation.
description: Build a CSS-only loading spinner using @keyframes. It should be a circular spinner with a colored arc that rotates.
correct_answer:
HTML:
CSS: .spinner { width: 40px; height: 40px; border: 4px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.1); border-left-color: #3498db; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 1s linear infinite; } @keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }
